_comment: WC2026 Group G, Matchday 2 (BC Place, Vancouver). MD1: Egypt 1-1 Belgium (great display, leading almost throughout); NZ 2-2 Iran (Elijah Just x2). All on 1 point. Odds Egypt 1.60 / Draw 4.0 / NZ 5.5. MOST EVEN match in the group. Calibrated in line with the market (Spain/Belgium lesson: don't go contrarian). REFEREE AND XI NOT YET CONFIRMED -> cards as placeholder.

meta.stakes: Group G with all 4 on 1 point. Egypt (Salah) chasing their 1st World Cup win in 9 attempts after a great 1-1 with Belgium; a gutsy NZ (2-2 with Iran) also goes for its 1st win. A win leaves either side very close to the round of 16.

home.name: Egypt
notes: Salah + Trezeguet; went toe to toe with Belgium (14 shots, led almost throughout). attack 1.25.
Calibrated in line with the market (~59%): Spain/Belgium lesson, don't underrate the favorite based on my bottom-up.
Solid defense: held off Belgium to the end -> defense 0.80.

away.name: New Zealand
notes: Physical, dangerous from set pieces; Elijah Just scored 2 against Iran. Does NOT sit as deep as Iran -> more open match (rho -0.05). attack 0.85.
Weak defense: leaked twice against Iran -> defense 1.12.
82nd in the FIFA ranking: the weakest in the group, but competitive.

cards._referee: Omar Al Ali (UAE), CONFIRMED - World Cup debut, AFC referee. Career ~4.08 YC/match (and a notable 0.26 reds/match). International-context discount; small bump for a physical, competitive NZ (Wood/set pieces), not a lopsided block. XI confirmed: Egypt with Salah + Marmoush, NZ with Wood + Just.
